fields.description:
## What

The root `README.md` documents the session-log command without its required
subcommand, which the CLI rejects — a P0 onboarding defect that directly
contradicts `docs/user-guide.md`.

- `README.md:333` shows `rebar session-log "<entry>"` (no subcommand).
- The CLI (`src/rebar/_commands/session_log.py`) consumes the first positional as the
  verb and errors with `unknown session-log action` for anything but `append`/`start`.
- `docs/user-guide.md:170-171` already shows the correct `rebar session-log append
  "<entry>"` / `rebar session-log start` forms.

## Acceptance Criteria
The P0 defect was the README showing the INVALID bare `rebar session-log "<entry>"` form.
This ticket corrected it in the README CLI catalog; sibling `b5a4` (same epic) then moved
that catalog into the generated `docs/cli-reference.md` (linked from README's `## CLI`),
so the always-current valid form now lives there — see the `supersedes` link. Final,
landed acceptance:
- [ ] The invalid bare `rebar session-log "<entry>"` form appears in NO doc (the P0
      onboarding defect is gone). Verify: `grep -rn 'session-log "' README.md docs/` finds
      nothing.
- [ ] The valid `session-log <append|start>` form is documented and copy-pasteable in the
      generated `docs/cli-reference.md` (`### session-log`, from the CLI's own help) and in
      `docs/user-guide.md` — and runs without the `unknown session-log action` error.
- [ ] `README.md`'s `## CLI` section links to `docs/cli-reference.md` where the corrected
      command reference (incl. session-log) now lives.

Files: `README.md` (line ~333).
